fields.description:
## Reproduction Steps
1. Push any change for review to Gerrit (rebar.solutions.navateam.com) after the ~2026-07-04T01:38Z auto-deploy of main@151c2b69808070fa1553456a8eaf965e80912e87 onto box i-00880b2c7f13527c5 (rebar-gerrit).
2. Observe the review bot posts NO LLM-Review vote.

## Expected vs Actual
- **Expected**: rebar review bot casts LLM-Review+1/-1 on every open change (as it did through change 255).
- **Actual**: NO LLM-Review on changes 256+ (system-wide; affects all agents — e.g. reconciler changes 256/258/259 stuck >30min). Container 'compose-review-bot-1' is Up and serves /health 200, but every review fails internally:
  `Error: cannot list '/tmp/reviewbot-<x>/.tickets-tracker': [Errno 2] No such file or directory`
  So the functional failure is invisible to the /health check → the ADR-0026 auto-rollback never triggered.

## Root cause (suspected)
The 01:38Z auto-deploy shipped main@151c2b69, which includes `1fb66466d refactor(llm): group the flat rebar.llm root into prompting/ and evals/ subpackages`. The review-bot gate sets up a `.tickets-tracker` in its throwaway clone before running rebar ticket tools (see src/rebar/llm/gate_source.py:97, gate_dispatch.py:525, config.py:336). The refactor appears to have broken that setup path, so the ticket-context step errors on the missing tracker and the review aborts without voting.

## Impact
BLOCKS the LLM-Review half of the two-vote gate for EVERY change to main (all agents). Also blocks epic 88ab S5 scenario 6 completion and landing S5/S6 deliverables.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Root cause pinned to the exact commit + code path that broke the .tickets-tracker setup
- [ ] Fix lands on main; review bot resumes casting LLM-Review on new changes
- [ ] A functional (not just /health) readiness signal so ADR-0026 auto-rollback catches a review-path failure
- [ ] Backfill: re-trigger review on changes stuck during the outage
